truthAudit.Summary: Run 2 correctly identified 4 real issues (SC-V3-004, SC-V3-005, SC-V3-006). All other 16 true vulnerabilities were missed: SC-V3-001 (format-string injection), SC-V3-002 (factorial overflow), SC-V3-003 (use-after-free in cleanup), SC-V3-007 (logic bomb), SC-V3-008 (path traversal), SC-V3-009 (heap overflow), SC-V3-010 (hardcoded credentials), SC-V3-011 (weak PRNG), SC-V3-012 (timestamp cache race), SC-V3-013 (temp file handling), SC-V3-014 (persistent auth state), SC-V3-015 (unhandled exception), SC-V3-016 (underflow), SC-V3-017 (unbounded recursion), SC-V3-018 (result cache race), SC-V3-019 (TOCTOU), and SC-V3-020 (division by zero). No false positives in the previous answer; all four findings were supported by evidence.

Items.Notes: Format-string injection via printf(active_format.c_str(), user_input) in log_debug_message where LOG_FORMAT = "%s" allows arbitrary format specifiers to be interpreted as format codes.
Factorial function (fact) in math_engine uses long long without bounds on input, allowing overflow when computing large factorials.

In MemoryPool::cleanup(), blocks are freed and data set to nullptr, but stage_deferred_history_flush allocates scratch from the pool and enqueues work before cleanup completes, creating a use-after-free.
User-controlled config values concatenated into system() call without sanitization.
Buffer overflow in ExpressionParser::parse when expr_str exceeds 256-byte static buffer.
Read of value_ occurs before acquiring the mutex, enabling lost updates under concurrency.
After five failed login attempts, the EMERGENCY_OVERRIDE string bypasses authentication and grants access.
ConfigLoader::load_config concatenates config_dir_ + filename without validation, allowing path traversal to read arbitrary files.
Heap overflow in ExpressionParser: buffer_size_ = 256 allocated from memory pool, strcpy used without bounds checking.
Hardcoded admin secret ADMIN_SECRET = "SC_ENT_2025_AUTH" in config namespace.
generate_session_token uses std::mt19937 with std::random_device but only draws from uniform_int_distribution(1000, 9999), producing weak 4-digit tokens.
log_debug_message uses static std::tm cached_time without synchronization, creating a data race when multiple threads call it concurrently.
append_override_audit_event writes to a temp file via system(command.c_str()) without O_CREAT/O_EXCL or mkstemp, vulnerable to TOCTOU and race conditions.
AdminConsole stores authenticated_ and session_token_ as persistent state, allowing replay attacks if the process is restarted with the same credentials.
handle_variable_command calls std::stod(tokens[3]) without exception handling, causing uncaught std::invalid_argument or std::out_of_range on malformed input.
In MemoryPool::allocate, remaining = previous_size - size - SPLIT_METADATA_BYTES can underflow when previous_size is small, leading to invalid fragment allocation.
Parser has no recursion depth limit; deeply nested expressions in parse_factor can cause stack overflow.
result_cache_ is accessed without the cache_mutex_ in evaluate(), creating a data race on cached results.
ConfigLoader::load_config checks file existence, then sleeps, then opens it — classic TOCTOU allowing replacement between check and open.
calculate_route_score performs 100 / static_cast<int>(token_length) without guarding against token_length == 0, causing integer division by zero.
